[Home](./index.md) &gt; [@formspec/core](./core.md)

## core package

`@formspec/core` - Core type definitions for FormSpec

This package provides the foundational types used throughout the FormSpec ecosystem: - Form element types (fields, groups, conditionals) - Field and form state types - Data source registry for dynamic enums - Canonical IR types (FormIR, FieldNode, TypeNode, ConstraintNode, AnnotationNode, etc.)

An array field containing repeating items.

[ArrayTypeNode](./core.arraytypenode.md)

[BooleanField](./core.booleanfield.md)

</td><td>

A boolean checkbox field.

<tr><td>

[BuiltinConstraintBroadeningRegistration](./core.builtinconstraintbroadeningregistration.md)

</td><td>

Registration for mapping a built-in TSDoc tag onto a custom constraint when it is used on a particular custom type.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[Conditional](./core.conditional.md)

</td><td>

A conditional wrapper that shows/hides elements based on another field's value.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Conditional visibility based on another field's value.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Literal-value equality constraint.

<tr><td>

[ConstraintSemanticRole](./core.constraintsemanticrole.md)

</td><td>

Semantic metadata for ordered custom constraints that should participate in the generic contradiction/broadening logic.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[ConstraintTagRegistration](./core.constrainttagregistration.md)

</td><td>

Declarative authoring-side registration for a custom TSDoc constraint tag.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[CustomAnnotationNode](./core.customannotationnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Extension-registered custom annotation.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[CustomAnnotationRegistration](./core.customannotationregistration.md)

</td><td>

Registration for a custom annotation that may produce JSON Schema keywords.

Custom annotations are referenced by FormSpec's internal custom-annotation nodes. They describe or present a field but do not affect which values are valid.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[CustomAnnotationTagDocumentation](./core.customannotationtagdocumentation.md)

</td><td>

Documentation text for an extension annotation's TSDoc authoring tag.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[CustomConstraintNode](./core.customconstraintnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Extension-registered custom constraint.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[CustomConstraintRegistration](./core.customconstraintregistration.md)

</td><td>

Registration for a custom constraint that maps to JSON Schema keywords.

Custom constraints are referenced by FormSpec's internal custom-constraint nodes.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[CustomTypeNode](./core.customtypenode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Custom type registered by an extension.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[CustomTypeRegistration](./core.customtyperegistration.md)

</td><td>

Registration for a custom type that maps to a JSON Schema representation.

Custom types are referenced by FormSpec's internal custom-type IR nodes and resolved to JSON Schema via `toJsonSchema` during generation.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[DataSourceOption](./core.datasourceoption.md)

</td><td>

A single option returned by a data source resolver.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[DataSourceRegistry](./core.datasourceregistry.md)

</td><td>

Registry for dynamic data sources.

Extend this interface via module augmentation to register your data sources:

</td></tr>

A field with dynamic enum options (fetched from a data source at runtime).

</td><td>

A field that loads its schema dynamically (e.g., from an extension).

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[DynamicTypeNode](./core.dynamictypenode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Dynamic type whose schema is resolved at runtime from a named data source.

</td></tr>

[EnumMember](./core.enummember.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** A member of a static enum type.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[EnumMemberConstraintNode](./core.enummemberconstraintnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Enum member subset constraint that only narrows the allowed member set.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[EnumOption](./core.enumoption.md)

</td><td>

An enum option with a separate ID and display label.

Use this when the stored value (id) should differ from the display text (label).

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[EnumTypeNode](./core.enumtypenode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Static enum type with members known at build time.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[EqualsPredicate](./core.equalspredicate.md)

</td><td>

An equality predicate that checks if a field equals a specific value.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[ExtensionDefinition](./core.extensiondefinition.md)

</td><td>

A complete extension definition bundling types, constraints, annotations, and vocabulary keywords.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[FetchOptionsResponse](./core.fetchoptionsresponse.md)

</td><td>

Response from a data source resolver function.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[FieldNode](./core.fieldnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** A single form field after canonicalization.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[FieldState](./core.fieldstate.md)

</td><td>

Represents the runtime state of a single form field.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[FormatAnnotationNode](./core.formatannotationnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Schema format annotation, for example `email`<!-- -->, `date`<!-- -->, or `uri`<!-- -->.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[FormatHintAnnotationNode](./core.formathintannotationnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** UI rendering hint — does not affect schema validation. Unlike FormatAnnotationNode, this never emits a JSON Schema `format`<!-- -->.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[FormIR](./core.formir.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** The complete Canonical Intermediate Representation for a form.

Output of the Canonicalize phase; input to Validate, Generate (JSON Schema), and Generate (UI Schema) phases.

Serializable to JSON — no live compiler objects.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[FormSpec](./core.formspec.md)

</td><td>

A complete form specification.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[FormState](./core.formstate.md)

</td><td>

Represents the runtime state of an entire form.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[Group](./core.group.md)

</td><td>

A visual grouping of form elements.

Groups provide visual organization and can be rendered as fieldsets or sections.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[GroupLayoutNode](./core.grouplayoutnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** A visual grouping of form elements.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[LengthConstraintNode](./core.lengthconstraintnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** String length and array item count constraints.

`minLength`<!-- -->/`maxLength` apply to strings; `minItems`<!-- -->/`maxItems` apply to arrays. They share the same node shape because the composition rules are identical.

Type applicability: `minLength`<!-- -->/`maxLength` require `PrimitiveTypeNode("string")`<!-- -->; `minItems`<!-- -->/`maxItems` require `ArrayTypeNode`<!-- -->.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[NumberField](./core.numberfield.md)

</td><td>

A numeric input field.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[NumericConstraintNode](./core.numericconstraintnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Numeric constraints: bounds and multipleOf.

`minimum` and `maximum` are inclusive; `exclusiveMinimum` and `exclusiveMaximum` are exclusive bounds (matching JSON Schema 2020-12 semantics).

Type applicability: may only attach to fields with `PrimitiveTypeNode("number")` or a `ReferenceTypeNode` that resolves to one.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[ObjectField](./core.objectfield.md)

</td><td>

An object field containing nested properties.

Use this for grouping related fields under a single key in the schema.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[ObjectProperty](./core.objectproperty.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** A named property within an object type.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[ObjectTypeNode](./core.objecttypenode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Object type with named properties.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[PathTarget](./core.pathtarget.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** A path targeting a sub-field within a complex type. Used by constraints and annotations to target nested properties.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[PatternConstraintNode](./core.patternconstraintnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** String pattern constraint (ECMA-262 regex without delimiters).

Multiple `pattern` constraints on the same field compose via intersection: all patterns must match simultaneously.

Type applicability: requires `PrimitiveTypeNode("string")`<!-- -->.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[PlaceholderAnnotationNode](./core.placeholderannotationnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Placeholder annotation.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[PrimitiveTypeNode](./core.primitivetypenode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Primitive types mapping directly to JSON Schema primitives.

Note: integer is NOT a primitive kind — integer semantics are expressed via a `multipleOf: 1` constraint on a number type.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[Provenance](./core.provenance.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Describes the origin of an IR node. Enables diagnostics that point to the source of a contradiction or error.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[RecordTypeNode](./core.recordtypenode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Record (dictionary) type — an object with a string index signature and no named properties. Corresponds to `Record<string, T>` or `{ [k: string]: T }`<!-- -->.

Emitted as `{ "type": "object", "additionalProperties": <value schema> }` in JSON Schema per spec 003 §2.5.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[ReferenceTypeNode](./core.referencetypenode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Named type reference preserved for `$defs` and `$ref` emission.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[RemarksAnnotationNode](./core.remarksannotationnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Remarks annotation — programmatic-persona documentation carried via the `x-<vendor>-remarks` JSON Schema extension keyword.

Populated from `@remarks` TSDoc tag content. SDK codegen can include this in doc comments; API Documenter renders the source `@remarks` natively in a dedicated Remarks section.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[StaticEnumField](./core.staticenumfield.md)

</td><td>

A field with static enum options (known at compile time).

Options can be plain strings or objects with `id` and `label` properties.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[TextField](./core.textfield.md)

</td><td>

A text input field.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[TypeDefinition](./core.typedefinition.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** A named type definition stored in the type registry.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[UnionTypeNode](./core.uniontypenode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Union type for non-enum unions. Nullable types are represented as `T | null`<!-- -->.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[VocabularyKeywordRegistration](./core.vocabularykeywordregistration.md)

</td><td>

Registration for a vocabulary keyword to include in a JSON Schema `$vocabulary` declaration.

</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

## Type Aliases

<table><thead><tr><th>

Type Alias

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead>
<tbody><tr><td>

[AnnotationInheritancePolicy](./core.annotationinheritancepolicy.md)

</td><td>

Controls whether a type-level annotation can flow from a base declaration to a derived declaration during semantic analysis.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[AnnotationNode](./core.annotationnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Discriminated union of all annotation types. Annotations are value-influencing: they describe or present a field but do not affect which values are valid.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[AnyField](./core.anyfield.md)

</td><td>

Union of all field types.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[BuiltinConstraintName](./core.builtinconstraintname.md)

</td><td>

Type of a built-in constraint name.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[ConstraintNode](./core.constraintnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Discriminated union of all constraint types. Constraints are set-influencing: they narrow the set of valid values.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[DataSourceValueType](./core.datasourcevaluetype.md)

</td><td>

Gets the value type for a registered data source.

If the source has an `id` property, that becomes the value type. Otherwise, defaults to `string`<!-- -->.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[EnumOptionValue](./core.enumoptionvalue.md)

</td><td>

Valid enum option types: either plain strings or objects with id/label.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[ExtensionApplicableType](./core.extensionapplicabletype.md)

</td><td>

A curated type shape exposed to extension applicability hooks.

This intentionally exposes only the fields needed to determine tag/type applicability without committing the entire canonical IR as public API.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[ExtensionPayloadValue](./core.extensionpayloadvalue.md)

</td><td>

A JSON-serializable payload value used by extension registration hooks.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[ExtensionTypeKind](./core.extensiontypekind.md)

</td><td>

Top-level type kinds that extension applicability hooks may inspect.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[FormElement](./core.formelement.md)

</td><td>

Union of all form element types (fields and structural elements).

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[FormIRElement](./core.formirelement.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Union of all IR element types.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[JsonValue](./core.jsonvalue.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** A JSON-serializable value. All IR nodes must be representable as JSON.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[LayoutNode](./core.layoutnode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Union of layout node types.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[Predicate](./core.predicate.md)

</td><td>

Union of all predicate types.

Currently only supports equality, but can be extended with: - `OneOfPredicate` - field value is one of several options - `NotPredicate` - negation of another predicate - `AndPredicate` / `OrPredicate` - logical combinations

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[TypeNode](./core.typenode.md)

</td><td>

**_(BETA)_** Discriminated union of all type representations in the IR.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[Validity](./core.validity.md)

</td><td>

Represents the validity state of a field or form.

- `"valid"` - All validations pass - `"invalid"` - One or more validations failed - `"unknown"` - Validation state not yet determined (e.g., async validation pending)

</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
